title: Code Review Finding
description: Structured output from the Reviewer role using the review-code skill. Emitted per change set or PR. Consumed by developers (for fixes), Technical Lead (for delivery readiness), Agent Coordinator (as phase gate evidence), and QA (for validation gaps). One document covers the full review; findings[] contains individual issues.

change_ref.description: PR number, commit SHA, or change description being reviewed.

delivery_plan_ref.description: technical-delivery-plan.json plan_id this review is part of.

implementation_result_ref.description: implementation-result.json slice_id that triggered this review.

change_type.description: Nature of the change being reviewed.

findings.description: Individual review findings. Empty array if no issues found.

file.description: Repo-relative path to the file containing the finding.

line.description: Line number where the finding starts (if applicable).

severity.description: blocking = must fix before merge; important = must fix before release; follow_up = track but not blocking; suggestion = advisory.

domain.description: Review matrix domain this finding falls under.

description.description: What the issue is, why it matters, and which code path is affected.

suggestion.description: Recommended fix or mitigation with enough specificity to be actionable.

confidence.description: How confident the reviewer is that this is actually a problem.

review_matrix.description: Summary status per review domain. All 8 domains must be explicitly checked (or skipped with justification).

blast_radius_assessment.description: Assessment of how far the change's effects extend beyond the changed files.

notes.description: What was checked and what was found.

merge_recommendation.description: Overall merge decision. Must be supported by review_matrix and findings evidence.

residual_risk.description: Remaining risk after merge that QA, SRE, or the next reviewer should monitor.

unrun_checks.description: Validation steps not run and the resulting risk exposure.

findings.description: Store courier lookup does not handle the case where the store-service API returns a 503. The current code will throw an unhandled exception that terminates the assignment command for all remaining orders in the batch.
findings.suggestion: Wrap store service call in try/catch; on 503 or connection error, fall back to warehouse assignment and log an error with order_id. Prevents single-store outage from blocking full batch.

blast_radius_assessment.notes: Non-COD path verified unchanged. Warehouse fallback path is shared with existing logic — both paths write to same assignments table with identical schema.

examples.residual_risk: Store service 503 handling not yet tested under load — should be covered by integration test in next QA cycle.
unrun_checks: Integration test against store-service stub under 503 condition
